title: +finger — live demo
description: Tour of the +finger character profile surface: viewing, setting fields, clearing, hiding, and miss handling.

steps.note: +finger with no argument shows the caller's own profile. Default fields appear in a fixed order even when empty so the layout reads cleanly.
Pass a name to look someone else up. The lookup matches on character name first, then alias.
+finger/set <field>=<value> stores arbitrary fields. Underscores and casing in field names are normalised.

steps.label: alias now shows in the profile

steps.note: Any field name works. Custom fields appear after the defaults, sorted alphabetically.
An empty value on the right of the = removes the field entirely.
Setting a value to @@ hides the field from display without forgetting it. Useful when you want a default field suppressed rather than blank.
If no player or alias matches, the command tells you so plainly.
